notes: exp29: MLX_MAX_MB_PER_BUFFER=2 MLX_MAX_OPS_PER_BUFFER=2 gives 58% peak memory reduction (3319→1407MB) with no latency penalty. Latency unchanged across all buffer sizes (thermal-controlled A/B test). Memory scales linearly with buffer limits: MB=8→2099MB, MB=16→2709MB, MB=32→3319MB (default), MB=128→4706MB. OPS=2 saves additional 318MB vs default OPS. No code changes — env var only.

exp30: mx.set_wired_limit() sweep at 0/512/1024/1536/2048/3072/4096 MB. No benefit at any value. Higher limits INCREASE latency (+5-10%), peak memory (+500-700MB), and variance. wired_limit=0 (default/disabled) is optimal. Hypothesis disproven — pinning weights in physical RAM hurts unified memory performance on Apple Silicon.

exp31: Bisected bf16 conversions for error contribution @1024. Current headroom: alpha=23.3%, fg=8.2% (critical). Top contributors: (1) backbone-bf16 contributes 21.3% of fg_final error — disabling raises fg headroom 8.2%->27.8%. (2) refiner-bf16 contributes 52.2% of alpha_final error but only 6.5% of fg_final. (3) decoder-bf16 contributes 5.0% alpha, 7.6% fg. Recommendation: if fg headroom becomes blocking, revert backbone_bf16_stages123 first — recovers 19.6% fg headroom with minimal latency cost. Refiner bf16 is safe to keep (alpha-dominated error). All-fp32 baseline: alpha=0.002, fg=0.006 (95%+ headroom).

exp32: Dropping pytorch_compatible=True from refiner GroupNorm. CATASTROPHIC fidelity failure: alpha_final=0.987, fg_final=0.973 (vs 0.050 threshold). Native MLX GroupNorm computes different normalization statistics — non-zero group means vs PyTorch's zero-mean guarantee. Not a weight permutation issue; fundamentally different reduction axes. Coarse outputs (pre-refiner) unaffected, confirming error is 100% from refiner GN. No weight remap can fix this. pytorch_compatible=True is REQUIRED for correctness.
exp33: Decoder 1x1 Conv2d→Linear bypass already implemented in fold_bn(). linear_fuse uses matmul via _fuse_weight_chunks, classifier uses mx.addmm. No conv2d dispatch in hot path. Nothing to do.

exp34: del backbone after feature extraction. Zero peak memory savings (3319.1MB both modes). Peak is set during backbone forward pass (attention intermediates), not from backbone weights coexisting with decoder activations. Deleting weights afterward doesn't lower the high-water mark. Hypothesis disproven.

exp35: _make_blend_weights_2d() already uses position=(yi>0, yi<last, xi>0, xi<last) — logically equivalent to EZ-CorridorKey _blend_weight(at_top, at_bottom, at_left, at_right). Image boundary edges get full weight, internal overlaps get linear ramps. Nothing to do.

exp39: Replaced x @ _fc_wt + bias with mx.addmm(bias, x, _fc_wt) in Hiera MLP (24 blocks x 2 layers). 440ms vs 422ms baseline = 4.3% regression. MLX compiler already fuses contiguous matmul+add; explicit addmm adds dispatch overhead. Original exp11 (912ms) likely had same issue compounded. Confirmed: mx.addmm not beneficial when operands are already contiguous pre-transposed arrays.

exp40: Pre-converting decoder/refiner weights to bf16 in safetensors. Only 3.4MB savings (decoder=5.6MB, refiner=1.2MB vs backbone=392MB). Negligible impact on load time or peak memory. Switched load path from safe_open(numpy) to mx.load (enables bf16 natively) — functionally correct, kept as minor infra improvement.

exp37: Padded stage 0 Linear K-dim from 112→128 (next 64-aligned) for 6 matmuls (QKV+proj+fc1 in blocks 0-1). Fidelity PASS but 1.9% latency regression (430.58ms vs 422.46ms baseline) and +140MB peak memory. Runtime mx.pad overhead (creating zero-padded tensors each forward pass) negates any Metal GEMM tile alignment benefit. Stage 0 is only 2 of 24 blocks — too small a fraction to offset padding cost. Hypothesis disproven for this architecture.

Exp 38 prereq. MLX streams show ZERO GPU-GPU overlap. Decoder-scale: 0.979x, backbone-scale: 0.990x, 4-matmul: 0.961x. Streams add 2-4% dispatch overhead. Apple Silicon command queues don't parallelize compute. Exp 38 abandoned.

Exp 39: fix mx.compile wrapping __call__. Split into compile-safe __call__ (no async_eval/stream) + forward_eager. Also fixed decoder pre-fold_bn fallback. Compiled 1024: 438.7ms (exact parity). Eager 1024: 439.2ms. Compiled was completely broken before (ValueError: Not allowed inside graph transformation).

Exp 41: tested 3 GN variants vs baseline. ContiguousCopyGN (+0.0 trick): -0.1% (noise). TransposedAffineGN (affine before 2nd transpose): +8.8% regression. TwoPassFP32GN (no transpose, mx.mean/var): +120% regression. Key finding: GN is 50% of refiner time at 1024 (107ms out of 215ms), much larger than 6.94% at 512 trace. mx.fast.layer_norm is irreplaceable — no Python-level alternative can match it. Custom Metal kernel is the only remaining path.

Exp 42: custom Metal kernel via mx.fast.metal_kernel (simd_sum + atomic_fetch_add). Two-kernel approach: stats kernel (sum+sumsq per group) + normalize kernel (affine+relu). Results: +41% slower than compiled nn.GroupNorm (9.82ms vs 6.95ms). Non-deterministic stats (drift=2.0 between runs). sumsq error=315 at 1024 (262K atomics per group). API lacks threadgroup shared memory — root cause. All GroupNorm optimization paths now exhausted.

name: tier2-metrics
hypothesis: Implement PSNR/SSIM/dtSSD in bench_video.py for Tier 2 fidelity gating
notes: Pure numpy windowed SSIM + dtSSD. V0 baseline: inf PSNR, 1.0 SSIM, 0.0 dtSSD (self-referential).

name: v1-ema-blending
hypothesis: Output-space EMA smoothing reduces flicker without fidelity loss
notes: All α (0.6-0.95) fail fidelity on motion video. α=0.95 passes Tier 2 but fails Tier 1. Inherent temporal lag.

name: v2-async-decode
hypothesis: Async decode overlap hides 43ms/frame CPU decode cost
notes: mx.async_eval + ThreadPoolExecutor decode-ahead. 7% wall-clock improvement. Less than theoretical — unified memory bandwidth contention.

name: v3-adaptive-tile-skip
hypothesis: Skip refiner on tiles with confident coarse alpha (near 0 or 1) to reduce compute
notes: Tile skip logic works correctly (zero additional error for skipped tiles). At tile_size=1024 on 2048 input: 0% skip rate — test video has alpha boundaries in all 4 quadrants. At tile_size=512 on 2048: 32-33% skip rate, 8% latency reduction (5552ms vs 6041ms), but tile_size=512 itself fails fidelity (max_abs=0.706/0.996, fg_psnr=13.3dB). Root cause: GroupNorm computes spatial stats per tile — smaller tiles = divergent normalization = boundary artifacts. This is independent of V3; it's a fundamental tiling limitation. Fix path: precompute GroupNorm stats on full image, then tile with frozen stats (GroupNorm-aware tiling).

name: v4-frozen-groupnorm
hypothesis: Precompute full-image GroupNorm stats before tiling to eliminate per-tile normalization artifacts, enabling tile_size=512
notes: FrozenGroupNorm works correctly — frozen-512 vs frozen-1024 = max_abs 0.0 (bit-identical). GroupNorm tiling artifact fully eliminated. BUT stats pass adds ~1300ms/frame (1 full refiner forward), making it 22% slower than unfrozen tile_size=1024 (7115ms vs 5799ms). At 512+tileskip=0.02: tile skip saves ~33% of tiles but stats pass overhead exceeds savings. Also discovered: tile skip has separate fg artifact (fg_max_abs=0.996) because refiner fg delta is non-zero even in confident-alpha regions. Frozen GN is correct but unprofitable at 2048 — only valuable on memory-constrained devices needing very small tiles.

name: metal-groupnorm-pipeline-impact
hypothesis: Metal GroupNorm v2 (-67% micro-bench) should improve real pipeline timing
notes: Pipeline timing unchanged at 2:06 (37 frames, 1920x1080). GroupNorm saves ~5ms/tile but 15 tiles x 227ms/tile means GN is ~2% of total. Backbone inference dominates.

name: v7-backbone-decoupled-validation
hypothesis: Running backbone at lower res (256-448) saves 12-38% latency while refiner recovers edge quality
notes: All backbone sizes fail fidelity on real frames. Even @448 (12% downscale) has alpha max_err=91/255. Errors on edges (hair, fingers). Matting is edge-sensitive — refiner cannot recover.

name: v5-partial-backbone-feature-reuse
hypothesis: Caching S2-S3 features (19/24 blocks) saves 73% backbone compute while maintaining quality
notes: S3-only acceptable (27.7/255 max) but only 1.6% speedup. S2+S3 caching has 247.5/255 max error — S2 features not temporally stable. Feature warping (V8) needed for S2 caching.

name: v6-refiner-tile-skip-frozen-gn
hypothesis: Frozen GN eliminates tiling artifacts and enables tile skip for 8-15% speedup
notes: Frozen GN + Metal kernel: 0.0 error vs non-tiled (perfect). But skip rate 0-25% on real content, stats collection overhead cancels savings. Net pipeline: 1636→1612ms (~0%). Keep for correctness, not speed.

name: env-var-tuning-real-pipeline
hypothesis: MLX_MAX_MB/OPS_PER_BUFFER tuning from 1024 experiment transfers to real pipeline
notes: MB=2/OPS=2 is 17% faster than default (1519 vs 1832ms). Small buffers force frequent eval which benefits tiled inference. Memory unchanged (762MB) since tiling already constrains peak.
